title: Cataract follow-up
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

red_flags.text: Lists reduced visual acuity, ciliary flush, photophobia, severe foreign-body sensation, corneal opacity, fixed pupil, severe headache with nausea, post-traumatic red eye, and immune compromise.
red_flags.rationale: Supports pain, red eye, sudden or reduced vision, and photophobia warning prompts in a broad red-eye differential.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Lists reduced visual acuity, ciliary flush, photophobia, severe foreign-body sensation, corneal opacity, fixed pupil, severe headache with nausea, post-traumatic red eye, and immune compromise.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Conjunctivitis

medication.text: Supports documenting expected visual changes, possible complication symptoms, activity or driving restrictions, contact arrangements, next appointment, vision change, spectacles, second-eye discussion, visual function, quality of life, and postoperative review processes without mapping medicines, doses, or autonomous urgency.
medication.rationale: Supports clinician-documented visual change, activity or driving discussion, spectacles, questions, next appointment, visual-function review, and follow-up without medicine details.
medication.evidence_extract: Supports documenting expected visual changes, possible complication symptoms, activity or driving restrictions, contact arrangements, next appointment, vision change, spectacles, second-eye discussion, visual function, quality of life, and postoperative review processes without mapping medicines, doses, or autonomous urgency.

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.1.5–1.1.6 and 1.9.1–1.9.3 — postoperative information and assessment
exact_section.locator: recommendations 1.1.5–1.1.6 and 1.9.1–1.9.3; official page lines 101–136 and 473–489

referral.text: Supports cataract identity, how cataract affects vision and quality of life, whether one or both eyes are affected, tailored information, and a preference-sensitive clinician discussion about cataract surgery without making a referral decision automatically.
referral.rationale: Supports cataract identity, reduced vision, functional or quality-of-life impact, laterality discussion, and clinician-documented cataract or surgery discussion.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports cataract identity, how cataract affects vision and quality of life, whether one or both eyes are affected, tailored information, and a preference-sensitive clinician discussion about cataract surgery without making a referral decision automatically.

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.2.2 — cataract information, visual function, quality of life, and referral discussion

referral.text: Supports clinician-led management, contact-lens hygiene or cessation discussion, response review, infection-control education, and referral for persistent symptoms, reduced acuity, photophobia, corneal opacity, painful red eye, severe headache with nausea, hyphema, trauma, or inability to open the eye; dose tables are not mapped.
referral.rationale: Supports clinician-documented earlier review when pain, visual change, or red-eye concerns worsen without automatic referral.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-led management, contact-lens hygiene or cessation discussion, response review, infection-control education, and referral for persistent symptoms, reduced acuity, photophobia, corneal opacity, painful red eye, severe headache with nausea, hyphema, trauma, or inability to open the eye; dose tables are not mapped.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Conjunctivitis

follow_up.text: Requires history and visual review for red eye and discharge, distinguishes discharge character, asks about vision, foreign-body sensation, photophobia, trauma and contact lenses, and highlights keratitis risk in contact-lens wearers.
follow_up.rationale: Supports visual review and assessed red-eye history without inferring a cataract complication.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Requires history and visual review for red eye and discharge, distinguishes discharge character, asks about vision, foreign-body sensation, photophobia, trauma and contact lenses, and highlights keratitis risk in contact-lens wearers.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Conjunctivitis

exact_section.heading: Section 7 — diagnosis, history, pitfalls, and contact-lens wearers

source_limitations: Blurred or cloudy vision wording, glare, halos, night-driving difficulty, lens opacity, red reflex, eye pressure, discharge, fixed three-to-six-month review, and the combined history draft remain unsupported.
The workflow must not diagnose cataract, infer complication or urgency, recommend surgery or referral, or add medicine or driving advice unless clinician-documented.
Qualified UAE clinician review has not been completed.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
